description: Character-level language model series progressing from bigram to transformer

key_decisions.reasoning: The dumbest possible model that still works. Establish a baseline loss that any better model must beat.
MLP → BatchNorm → RNN → attention → transformer. Each step adds exactly one new idea so you can isolate what each contributes.
Small enough to iterate fast, complex enough to be non-trivial. You can tell immediately if the model is learning.

lessons: Always establish the simplest baseline first — bigram model, linear model, whatever
Add complexity one piece at a time and measure the effect of each addition
BatchNorm is subtle — the initialization matters more than people think
